Dev Notes
Development Status
Internal notes for picking up where we left off. Last updated: March 20, 2026.

Working

  • ✓ Auth via Supabase (email+password, JWT tokens, auto-refresh every 50 min)
  • ✓ Sign Up creates Supabase user + GHL contact with tier tags
  • ✓ GHL tier lookup on login (dealflow-free / dealflow-academy / dealflow-alumni)
  • ✓ Auth gate on dashboard (redirects to login if no valid session)
  • ✓ Live deal data from Supabase opportunities + management_companies tables
  • ✓ Deal filtering, search, grid/list views, deal detail page with fee benchmarks
  • ✓ Buy Box wizard → Supabase user_buy_box + GHL contact custom fields
  • ✓ GP/Sponsor directory with profiles, track record, follow/unfollow
  • ✓ Deal Q&A system (Supabase deal_qa table)
  • ✓ Deck upload → Supabase Storage (deal-decks bucket) + auto-enrichment via Claude
  • ✓ User data sync: localStorage (primary) ↔ Supabase (persistent backup)
  • ✓ Goals ↔ GHL sync: Supabase goals push to GHL custom fields, GHL seeds Supabase on first login
  • ✓ Social proof: deal stage counts via deal_stage_counts view
  • ✓ Admin analytics dashboard (hot deals, funnel, top users)
  • ✓ Income Goal Bridge on dashboard (distribution-based progress tracking)
  • ✓ Deployed to dealflow.growyourcashflow.io via Vercel (auto-deploy on push to main)
  • ✓ Code on GitHub: git@github.com:PascalWagner/gyc-dealflow.git

Known Issues

  • ✗ GHL → Supabase sync is login-only — if a user's GHL contact is updated directly (e.g. by admin), changes only pull on next login
  • ✗ Magic link sign-in not built yet
  • ✗ Forgot password flow not built
  • ✗ Weekly digest email (kill-switched off, needs migration from old Airtable version)

Still To Do

  • ☑ GHL ↔ Supabase bidirectional goals sync (target income, capital, timeline → GHL custom fields)
  • ☐ Build magic link / forgot password flow via Supabase Auth
  • ☐ Subscription/payment integration (Stripe)
  • ☐ Deck review request flow
  • ☐ GP introduction request flow
  • ☐ Email notifications for new deals matching buy box
  • ☐ Weekly digest email (re-enable with Supabase backend)
  • ☑ Airtable backup folder + migration script deleted (migration complete)

Architecture

DatabaseSupabase (PostgreSQL) — 12 tables + 2 views
File StorageSupabase Storage (deal-decks bucket, signed URLs)
AuthSupabase Auth (JWT) + GHL tier lookup
CRMGoHighLevel (contacts, tags, buy box custom fields)
AI EnrichmentClaude API (deck/PPM text extraction → deal field mapping)
EmailResend (deck submission notifications)
AutomationsMake.com (Org 5180916 / Team 1371669) — deal processing pipelines
HostingVercel (serverless functions + static)
GitHubgit@github.com:PascalWagner/gyc-dealflow.git
Domaindealflow.growyourcashflow.io
Supabase Projectgyc-dealflow (ID: nntzqyufmtypfjpusflm)

Data Sync Architecture

DataPrimarySync TargetDirection
Deal StageslocalStorageSupabaseBidirectional (local-first)
PortfoliolocalStorageSupabaseBidirectional (local-first)
GoalslocalStorageSupabaseBidirectional (local-first)
Tax DocslocalStorageSupabaseBidirectional (local-first)
Buy BoxSupabaseGHL custom fieldsSupabase → GHL
DealsSupabase—Server only
SponsorsSupabase—Server only
Auth/TierSupabase AuthGHL tagsGHL → Supabase (on login)

Local-first pattern: User changes save to localStorage instantly, then fire-and-forget sync to Supabase. On login, Supabase data merges into localStorage (fills gaps, never overwrites).

Migration Notes (March 2026)

  • Full migration from Airtable → Supabase completed March 19-20, 2026
  • All API endpoints now query Supabase exclusively — zero Airtable dependencies remain
  • Airtable backup folder + migration script fully removed
  • Make.com still used for deal processing automations (GDrive → enrichment pipeline)
  • Deck/PPM files stored in Supabase Storage deal-decks bucket (signed URLs, 1-year expiry)

Network & Moat Strategy
Building defensible network effects. The moat is the people, not the software.
Introduction Analytics
0
Total Intros
0
Unique GPs
0
Unique LPs
0
This Month
No introduction requests tracked yet. When LPs request intros from deal pages, they’ll appear here.
Network Effects Playbook
1. LP Network Lock-In
More LPs → more intro requests → GPs get warm leads for free → GPs list more deals → more LPs join. Track: LP signups, active users, intros requested per month.
2. GP Recruitment via Data
Show GPs not on the platform: “We made X intros that led to $Y in capital deployed through our network.” Prove value with data, then onboard them. Track: capital attributed to intros, GP conversion rate.
3. Data Moat
Every deal reviewed, every DD checklist completed, every buy box configured creates proprietary data. Competitor would need to replicate the database AND the community. Track: deals in DB, DD completions, buy box profiles.
4. Academy as Trust Engine
Academy members get intros, deal access, DD tools. Non-members see deals but can’t act. Upsell path: free browse → consultation → Academy → Fund LP. Track: free-to-paid conversion, Academy retention.
Key Metrics to Track
MetricCurrentTargetWhy It Matters
Active LPs--500Critical mass for GP interest
GP Partners--50Supply side of marketplace
Intros / Month--25Engagement proof for GPs
Capital Attributed$0$5MProves platform value
Deals in Database--500Content moat depth
Transaction Strategy
Roadmap to facilitate capital flow and capture transaction revenue.
Phase 1: Intro & Attribution (Now)
What: Facilitate LP-to-GP introductions. Track which intros lead to investments.
Revenue: None directly. Build attribution data to prove platform value.
Build:
  • ✓ “Request Introduction” button on deal pages
  • ✓ Track intro requests (localStorage + analytics events)
  • □ Follow-up system: after intro, ask LP “did you invest?”
  • □ Attribution dashboard: intros → meetings → investments → $ deployed
  • □ GP-facing report: “GYC sent you X qualified LPs”
Phase 2: Placement Agent (6-12 months)
What: Become a registered placement agent. Take a fee for capital raised through the platform.
Revenue: 1-2% placement fee on capital deployed via GYC intros.
Build:
  • □ Legal: BD registration / RIA considerations
  • □ GP agreements: formal placement terms
  • □ “Invest Now” button with commitment tracking
  • □ Capital commitment pipeline (soft circle → hard commit → funded)
  • □ Automated LP verification (accredited investor checks)
Phase 3: Transaction Layer (12-24 months)
What: Facilitate the actual money movement. Payment processing, subscription docs, capital calls.
Revenue: Transaction fees + platform fees. Take a cut of every dollar raised.
Build:
  • □ Digital subscription agreements (e-sign integration)
  • □ ACH / wire payment facilitation
  • □ Capital call management for GPs
  • □ Distribution tracking & reporting
  • □ K-1 document distribution
Phase 4: Secondary Market & Tokenization (24+ months)
What: Enable LP-to-LP secondary trades. Potentially tokenize LP interests on-chain.
Revenue: Transaction fees on secondary trades. Liquidity premium pricing.
Build:
  • □ Secondary marketplace: list LP interests for sale
  • □ Price discovery mechanism (NAV-based, auction, negotiated)
  • □ ROFR (Right of First Refusal) workflow for GPs
  • □ Blockchain tokenization of LP interests (ERC-1400 security tokens)
  • □ Smart contract-based distributions
  • □ ATS (Alternative Trading System) registration
Revenue Model Evolution
PhaseRevenue StreamPotential
Academy + FundSubscriptions + management fees$200K/yr
Placement Agent1-2% of capital placed$500K-2M/yr
Transaction LayerPayment + platform fees$1-5M/yr
Secondary MarketTrade fees + liquidity premium$5-20M/yr
